assertions.text: Output contains both a top-level kapp help block and a kapp deploy help block (labeled or visually distinct)
Top-level help lists all five subcommands: deploy, delete, status, logs, version
Top-level help shows a USAGE/synopsis line
Deploy help shows the positional manifest arg AND the - convention for stdin
Deploy help shows all four flags: --namespace/-n, --dry-run, --wait, --timeout (with default like 10m visible)
Deploy help has an EXAMPLES section that appears prominently (lead with examples per clig.dev)
Deploy help notes the prod-namespace confirmation behavior
Both blocks use visual section headings (bold or ALL-CAPS like USAGE/OPTIONS/EXAMPLES, heroku/git style)
Output is the raw help text only, with minimal or no surrounding commentary (the user said 'just give me the text')

prompt: Write the top-level `kapp --help` output and the `kapp deploy --help` output for an internal Kubernetes-style CLI we're building called `kapp`. Subcommands: deploy, delete, status, logs, version. Deploy takes a manifest file path positional arg (or `-` for stdin) and supports --namespace/-n, --dry-run, --wait, --timeout=10m. Requires confirmation before applying to prod namespaces (env-derived). Used both interactively by SREs and in GitHub Actions pipelines. Docs at docs.example.internal/kapp. Just give me the text.
